history: The client has a history of mobility issues and a past prolonged illness that led to immobility. They recently underwent shoulder surgery and are experiencing a challenging recovery process with persistent pain due to a herniated cervical disc. They have started a new relationship, which coincided with a resurgence of physical symptoms, suggesting a link between emotional stress and physical health.
intermediate_belief: If I cannot control my physical condition, then I am not in control of my life.
intermediate_belief_depression: If my physical condition doesn't improve, then my life will remain stagnant and unfulfilling.
coping_strategies: The client immerses themselves in mathematics as a refuge and source of intellectual stimulation, avoiding direct discussion of their physical pain. They focus on teaching and learning math to gain a sense of accomplishment and control.
situation: Received an MRI report indicating a herniated disc.
auto_thought: I can't believe this is happening again. My body is failing me.
behavior: The client contacted the therapist after receiving the MRI report. They avoid discussing their physical pain directly and instead focus on mathematical pursuits.
